input.question: How many courses have no prerequisites vs at least one?

payload.question: How many courses have no prerequisites vs at least one?

output.sql:
WITH prereq_counts AS (
  SELECT
    c.course_id AS course_id,
    COUNT(cp.prerequisite_course_id) AS prereq_count
  FROM courses AS c
  LEFT JOIN course_prerequisites AS cp
    ON c.course_id = cp.course_id
  GROUP BY c.course_id
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN prereq_count = 0 THEN 'No prerequisites' ELSE 'At least one prerequisite' END AS prerequisite_status,
  COUNT(*) AS course_count
FROM prereq_counts
GROUP BY prerequisite_status
ORDER BY course_count DESC;

visualization.title: Courses: No Prerequisites vs At Least One
visualization.description: Bar chart comparing the number of courses with no prerequisites versus those with at least one prerequisite.

payload.sql:
WITH prereq_counts AS (
  SELECT
    c.course_id AS course_id,
    COUNT(cp.prerequisite_course_id) AS prereq_count
  FROM courses AS c
  LEFT JOIN course_prerequisites AS cp
    ON c.course_id = cp.course_id
  GROUP BY c.course_id
)
SELECT
  CASE WHEN prereq_count = 0 THEN 'No prerequisites' ELSE 'At least one prerequisite' END AS prerequisite_status,
  COUNT(*) AS course_count
FROM prereq_counts
GROUP BY prerequisite_status
ORDER BY course_count DESC;
